title: [P1] Define production observability dashboards, metrics taxonomy, and alerts
acceptance_criteria: `MetricsAndDashboards.md` defines every production metric, unit, labels, owner, and consumer.
All new metrics use the shared registry and reject non-allowlisted labels.
No workflow/run/step/session/user/repository/artifact identity appears in metric labels.
Legacy StatsD metrics have an explicit migration or removal mapping.
Repo-owned dashboards cover API, Temporal/workers, workflows/steps, providers/profiles/cost, Omnigent bridge/hosts, artifacts/Live Logs/Chat, and safety/intervention.
SLOs have explicit SLIs, exclusions, windows, objectives, owners, and calibration status.
Paging alerts use multi-window burn or another documented evidence-based threshold and include working runbooks.
Alerts distinguish user/policy errors from service failures and avoid paging on isolated run failures.
Dashboard drill-down links are authorized, template-controlled, and compatible with #2559 trace/log projections.
Cost/token dashboards consume the durable attribution semantics from #2560.
CI validates metrics, cardinality budgets, dashboards, rule syntax, alert annotations, and runbook links.
A combined-stack test demonstrates scrape/export, dashboard query inputs, and selected alert evaluation.
Production rollout documents expected series count, telemetry overhead, data retention, and rollback.
source_resolution: Recovered complete issue body through authenticated GitHub issue tooling after trusted workflow context identified truncation.
